entity_name: Top 24 Electric Power Companies in Georgia
canonical_name: Top 24 Electric Power Companies in Georgia
facts: The electric power industry in Georgia consists of a mix of utilities, contractors, and engineering firms dedicated to providing reliable energy solutions.
Companies in the sector are increasingly investing in smart grid technologies and energy efficiency measures while adapting to changing regulations and consumer demands.
Georgia has gained recognition for its push towards sustainability with significant growth in solar energy projects.
The list includes companies ranging from small firms with fewer than 50 employees to large organizations employing over 10,000.
Many of these companies are headquartered in key cities like Atlanta and Marietta.

Diverse Power Incorporated is a private electric utility cooperative based in LaGrange, Georgia, founded in 1936 that serves over 27,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers across several counties in Georgia and Alabama.
Diverse Power Incorporated offers programs and initiatives for electric vehicles and solar energy alongside its traditional electricity services.

GDS Associates, Inc. is a consulting and engineering firm based in Marietta, Georgia, founded in 1986 with approximately 229 employees that provides energy supply planning, regulatory consulting, renewable energy resources, and distributed generation solutions.

EnerVision, Inc. is an Atlanta-based consulting firm founded in 1998 with 27 professionals that specializes in power supply planning, renewable energy integration, and demand-side management for electric utilities.

Renewvia Energy Corporation is a private Atlanta-based company founded in 2008 that provides solar engineering, procurement, construction (EPC), and electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure services.
Renewvia Energy secured $15.0 million in debt funding in November 2023 to expand its utility-scale solar and storage pipeline.

Gresco Utility Supply, Inc. is a Forsyth, Georgia-based company founded in 1960 with approximately 152 employees that supplies EV charging solutions, LED lighting, solar products, and logistical support to utilities and contractors.

RavenVolt Inc., an ABM Company headquartered in Cumming, Georgia, was founded in 2020 to specialize in turn-key microgrid systems and utility battery solutions for commercial, industrial, and municipal clients.
RavenVolt Inc received $170.0 million in funding in August 2022 to support its mission of reducing emissions and improving grid resilience.

CS HURD Electrical Contracting is a Savannah, Georgia-based firm founded in 1999 with 51-200 employees that specializes in sustainable energy solutions including solar panel installations for residential, commercial, and industrial clients.

ABB Enterprise Software is an Atlanta-based technology company founded in 1988 that employs over 10,000 people globally and provides electrification, automation, advanced control systems, power converters, and industrial software for utilities and manufacturing.

FieldCore is an Atlanta-based field services organization founded in 2017 with over 10,000 employees that operates as part of GE Vernova's Gas Power One Field Services team focusing on decarbonization and electrification efforts.

Current Edge Solutions is a Savannah, Georgia-based service-disabled veteran-owned engineering firm founded in 2019 with 37 employees that provides capabilities in power generation, renewable energy systems, and microgrid development.

Southwire Company, LLC is a family-owned Carrollton, Georgia manufacturer founded in 1950 with 5001-10000 employees that produces wire and cable, tools, and power management solutions for utilities, construction, and renewable energy sectors.

Indigo Energy is an Atlanta-based private energy supplier founded in 2008 with approximately 53 employees that provides solar energy solutions, electric vehicle charging infrastructure, and EV charging consulting services to commercial fleets and government entities.

Enercon Services, Inc. is a Kennesaw, Georgia-based engineering and environmental consulting firm founded in 1983 with 1001-5000 employees that offers expertise in nuclear power, renewable energy sources like solar and wind, and electric vehicle infrastructure.
Enercon Services, Inc. acquired Power Centric Solutions to strengthen its engineering consulting capabilities for electrical utilities.

1910 Legacy Enterprises is an Atlanta-based company founded in 1910 with around 16 employees that operates Axis Energy, a subsidiary dedicated to renewable energy solutions including engineering, procurement, construction (EPC), and operations and maintenance (O&M) services.

PSG Energy Services is a Macon, Georgia-based energy solutions provider founded in 2006 that focuses on renewable energy, building efficiency, electrification, commercial solar installations, and intelligent HVAC controls.

Solar Energy Partners is a Smyrna, Georgia-based company founded in 2017 with approximately 29 employees that provides residential solar panel installation, solar batteries, and electric vehicle (EV) chargers primarily in South Carolina and Georgia.

Sentry Electrical Group, Inc. is a Lawrenceville, Georgia-based construction engineering firm founded in 2006 with approximately 133 employees that constructs transmission lines, substations, and solar photovoltaic installations, having completed over 550 miles of transmission lines.

A. West Enterprise is an Albany, Georgia-based electrical contractor founded in 2009 that specializes in medium-voltage electrical distribution and the installation of EV charging stations and generator setups across Georgia, Florida, Alabama, South Carolina, Tennessee, and North Carolina.

Mdsi (Hitachi Energy) is an Atlanta-based technology company founded in 1997 with around 131 employees that provides energy generation, transmission, distribution solutions, and electrical equipment manufacturing for utilities and renewable energy markets.

Capital City Electrical Services, LLC is an Atlanta-based private electrical services company founded in 2006 with 51-200 employees that specializes in residential and commercial electrical repairs, installations, and upgrades.

Patterson & Dewar Engineers, Inc. is a Norcross, Georgia-based engineering firm founded in 1947 with approximately 112 employees that designs and evaluates power delivery infrastructures including transmission systems, substations, distribution networks, and smart grid technologies for electric utility companies.
Inglett & Stubbs International, LLC is an Atlanta-based construction and engineering services firm founded in 2004 that specializes in EPC services for power generation and microgrids.

entity_name: Southeast US Electric Vehicle & Battery Supply Chain Projects
canonical_name: Southeast US Electric Vehicle & Battery Supply Chain Projects
facts: July 11, 2024 Oglethorpe Power announced its Board of Directors and 38 member cooperatives decided to invest about $2 billion constructing a two unit, roughly 1,200 mw total, highly efficient combined cycle natural gas fired generating plants in Forsyth Georgia.
July 11, 2024 Oglethorpe Power plans to add a simple-cycle combustion unit to their Box Springs facility in Talbot County Georgia.
November 11, 2022 Georgia Governor Kemp announced Freyr Battery is planning to invest about $1.6 billion in a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing plant in Newnan, Georgia (Coweta County).
November 11, 2022 Freyr Battery is calling the project “Giga America” and the first phase will be $1.7 billion.
November 11, 2022 The company plans to start engineering work on this project in a few months.
November 11, 2022 Georgia provided financial incentives to attract the development of this project.
November 24, 2022 Hyundai announced plans to invest nearly $1 billion ($926 million) in Bryan County Georgia to build a new manufacturing plant producing EV’s and EV powertrains.
November 24, 2022 Construction should start early in 2023 with production at the facility scheduled to begin in 2025.
November 24, 2022 This plant is expected to produce 900,000 EV Power systems and 450,000 integrated charging control units.
November 24, 2022 The Hyundai Mobis plant is located in Belfast Commerce Park in Richmond Hill Georgia.

December 14, 2022 Redwood Materials announced their plan to spend about $3.5 billion on a new battery materials facility, located at Camp Hall in Berkley County near the Port of Charleston, South Carolina.
December 14, 2022 They will call the site their next Battery Materials Campus.
December 14, 2022 The facility is expected to supply a network of electric vehicle (EV) battery plants stretching from Michigan to Georgia.
December 14, 2022 They hope to break ground on the facility early in 2023.
December 14, 2022 The facility will recycle, refine and manufacture anode and cathode components; that are currently only sourced outside of North America.

December 5, 2022 Ford Motor Company in a Joint Venture with South Korean company ‘SK On’ announced they were investing $5.8 billion and building 2 battery manufacturing plants in Glendale (Hardin County) Kentucky.
December 5, 2022 The Joint Venture (JV) is named ‘BlueOval SK’ and the production of electric vehicle (EV) batteries should begin by the end of 2025.

December 5, 2022 ‘BlueOval SK’ Battery Park will train new workers in necessary skills at a new Elizabethtown Community and Technical College (ECTC) Training Center, that will also be located at the BlueOval SK Battery Park.

December 5, 2022 As of December 5th 2022 Ford reports that 4.3 million cubic yards of soil have been moved, 283,000 tons of stone have been placed, 66,000 cubic yards of concrete have been poured, 3,300 tons of rebar have been tied, foundations have been laid and 7,900 tons of structural steel have been erected.
December 5, 2022 Barton Malow is the General Contractor, along with Gray Construction (Lexington KY).
December 5, 2022 Walbridge Construction is the lead construction contractor on the Blue Oval project in Stanton Kentucky.
December 2, 2022 GM and LG Energy solutions announced they were investing an additional $275 million to expand their battery manufacturing capacity in Spring Hill (Maury County) TN.
April 16, 2021 GM and LG Energy Solutions announced they were investing $2.3 billion to build an Electric Vehicle battery manufacturing facility in Spring Hill (Maury County) Tennessee.
April 16, 2021 This plant will provide batteries for Cadillac’s Lyriq vehicles and other GM Ultium platform electric vehicle models.
April 16, 2021 Battery production is expected to begin late in 2023.
May 31, 2023 Toyota announced it is investing $2.1 billion in its North Carolina battery manufacturing plant.
May 31, 2023 These batteries will power an all-new, large (three row) SUV to be assembled at its Georgetown Kentucky facilities, starting in 2025.
May 31, 2023 The new battery plant situated near Greensboro North Carolina, is already under construction, and will receive this additional $2.1 billion investment.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is building a $7.6 billion electric vehicle assembly campus in Ellabell, Bryan County.
The HMGMA megasite spans 2,996 acres with more than 16 million square feet of factory floor.
HMGMA targets 8,500 direct jobs and a 500,000-vehicle annual production capacity.
The adjacent HL-GA Battery Company joint venture between Hyundai and LG Energy Solution is a $4.3 billion full-scale plant.
As of mid-2024, seventeen announced suppliers committed approximately $2.5 billion in capital and roughly 6,900 additional jobs across twelve Georgia counties for the HMGMA cascade.
HMGMA hired 81 percent of its workforce from within a 60-mile radius per HMGMA Communications.
The Port of Savannah moved 5.7 million TEUs in fiscal year 2025, an 8.6 percent year-over-year gain.
Garden City Terminal remains the largest single-terminal container facility in North America.

Georgia Ports Authority completed $3.2 billion in port infrastructure over the past decade and announced a $4.5 billion ten-year plan including five big-ship berths, the Mason Mega Rail terminal, and inland ports.
Port-proximate Savannah industrial rents trade at approximately 55 percent above the broader U.S. average.
Atlanta emerged as the second-largest U.S. data center market with 1,459.2 megawatts operating against just 2 percent vacancy.
AWS announced an $11 billion Georgia expansion on January 7, 2025, creating at least 550 jobs in Butts and Douglas counties.
Microsoft, Google, Meta (in Newton County), QTS, Digital Realty, and DC BLOX are expanding in Atlanta's data center market.
Hyperscale users pre-leased over 300 megawatts in 2025 with another 600 megawatts expected.
The Georgia Public Service Commission approved Georgia Power's 2025 Integrated Resource Plan on July 15, 2025.
The PSC ordered a freeze of Georgia Power base rates through 2028 on July 31, 2025.
On December 19, 2025, the PSC approved an agreement allowing Georgia Power to add 9,885 megawatts of new generation (6,000-megawatt minimum, 8,500-megawatt maximum).
Georgia Power secured over 8 gigawatts of new generation capacity scheduled to come online after 2030.
Utility lead times for new substations approach four years in the most constrained corridors.
The PSC's January 2025 large-load tariff rule requires developers to demonstrate end-user commitments, zoning confirmation, and posted collateral for power procurement.
Atlanta industrial inventory exceeds 800 million square feet with approximately 18.9 million square feet under construction at year-end 2025.
Major industrial occupiers include Amazon, FedEx, UPS, The Home Depot (Cobb County HQ), and Lockheed Martin Aeronautics (Cobb County, F-22 and F-35 production).
Industrial feasibility analysis along the I-285 perimeter and I-75/I-85 outbound corridors must model HMGMA-driven supplier demand spillover from Savannah back through Macon into the metro.
Anovion Technologies in Bainbridge is building an $800 million synthetic graphite facility for EV batteries.
Aspen Aerogels in Statesboro announced a $1.1 billion project.
Hanwha Qcells in Cartersville is building a $2.5 billion solar panel manufacturing facility, the largest North American solar manufacturing facility.
SK Battery America in Commerce is building a $2.6 billion EV battery cell plant.
Plant Vogtle in Burke County is the only U.S. nuclear plant with four reactors totaling 4,536 megawatts; Units 3 and 4 entered commercial operation July 31, 2023 and April 29, 2024.
Combined Vogtle expansion cost reached approximately $34 to 36.8 billion, more than double the original $14 billion estimate.
Vogtle 3 added approximately $5.42 per month to the average Georgia Power residential bill; Vogtle 4 added approximately $8.95 per month.
Georgia Power serves approximately 2.7 million customers across 155 of 159 counties.
Forty-one Electric Membership Corporations serve approximately 5 million Georgia residents across 73 percent of the state's land area on 200,400 miles of distribution lines.

EMCs jointly own generation share of Plant Vogtle through Oglethorpe Power (30 percent) and Municipal Electric Authority of Georgia (22.7 percent), with Dalton Utilities at 1.6 percent and Georgia Power retaining 45.7 percent.
Walton EMC supplies Meta's Newton County data center on a 100 percent renewable basis.
Jackson EMC serves the I-85 NE corridor (Gwinnett through Banks counties) and competes for hyperscale loads.
Cobb EMC serves the Northwest Georgia region.
Any large-load deal (5 megawatts and above) requires an early service-territory analysis due to differing power lead times between Georgia Power and EMCs.

Pooler, Richmond Hill, Guyton, Rincon, and the broader Effingham-Bryan-Bulloch corridor multifamily and rental absorption now move with HMGMA hiring milestones rather than traditional Savannah MSA fundamentals.

Feasibility studies must phase rent and occupancy ramps to actual production milestones including October 2024 SOP IONIQ 5, March 2025 grand opening, and 2026 to 2027 ramp toward 500,000-unit annual capacity.
A September 2025 federal immigration enforcement event detained approximately 450 mostly South Korean workers at the HL-GA Battery construction site.

Georgia is the largest U.S. peanut producer, largest U.S. pecan producer, largest U.S. blueberry producer, one of the top three sweet onion producers (Vidalia), a major poultry processor (largest U.S. broiler-producing state), a leading cotton producer, and a major timber state with 24 million acres of working forestland.
Georgia's craft brewing and distilling cluster includes more than 130 active breweries plus the Atlanta-headquartered American Distilling Institute member roster.
Senate Bill 68 and Senate Bill 69 tort reform package was signed by Governor Brian Kemp on April 21, 2025.

The reforms eliminate phantom damages, narrow negligent-security liability to ordinary care, allow bifurcation of liability and damages in cases over $150,000, prohibit anchoring tactics in non-economic damages closing arguments, and require litigation financiers to register and disclose funding agreements above $25,000.
Georgia Office of the Commissioner of Insurance and Safety Fire's November 2024 report documented a 25 percent increase in claims volume driving commercial premium increases.
Commercial general liability, commercial auto, and habitational/multifamily premium re-rating typically lags statutory reform by 18 to 30 months.
Georgia Department of Community Affairs administers the Georgia Allocation System for private activity tax-exempt bonds with $1.526 billion in 2026 allocation capacity at $135 per capita.
SBA Georgia District Office in Atlanta is led by District Director Terri Denison and services all 159 counties.
USDA Rural Development maintains its Georgia State Office at the Stephens Federal Building in Athens, with State Director Joyce White and Deputy State Director Jared Hooper.
USDA operates six area offices in Barnesville, Blackshear, Camilla, Sandersville, Statesboro, Tifton, and Douglas, plus the Athens state office.

Director for Georgia Business and Cooperatives Programs is Samuel Kirkland, administering Business and Industry, Rural Energy for America Program, Community Facilities, and Value-Added Producer Grant lending.

facts: Assembly takes place at Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) in West Point, Georgia.
Manufacturing of most EV6 variants for the North American market is expected to move to Kia's West Point, Georgia assembly plant for the 2025 model year.
Production is expected to begin in early calendar year 2025.
Vehicles with the GT trim level will still be manufactured in Korea.
This is expected to make the Georgia-made EV6 models eligible for the US federal EV tax credit.
On Georgia-made vehicles, 2025 swaps the CCS charging port for the Tesla-style NACS / SAE J3400 port.
On Georgia-made vehicles, the charge port location is moved from the rear passenger (right-hand) side to the rear driver (left-hand) side of the car.

facts: ABC Technologies completed its acquisition of TI Fluid Systems plc on April 15, 2025, with the combined business rebranding as TI Automotive.
Terry Campbell was appointed President & Chief Executive Officer and Alexander De Bock was appointed Chief Financial Officer for the combined group.
Julien Plenchette joined as President for the Americas effective April 8, 2024, based in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
TI Fluid Systems signed a Collaboration Agreement with Sanden to accelerate next-generation thermal refrigerant modules, complementing TIFS's capabilities with Sanden's e-compressors.
TI Fluid Systems completed its acquisition of Cascade Engineering Europe (CEE), a leader in designing and manufacturing thermal fluid connectors and complex plastic components.
TI Fluid Systems held an opening ceremony for its strategic growth investment facility in Shanghai, China on October 26, 2023.
Janis N. Acosta was appointed Chief Legal Officer & Company Secretary, bringing over 20 years of global tier-one automotive supply experience.
TI Fluid Systems will open new e-Mobility Innovation Centers (eMICs) in Nagoya, Japan and Incheon, South Korea during the summer to promote collaboration.
TI Fluid Systems is positioned to support North American customers fulfilling battery eligibility requirements of the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
TI Fluid Systems finalized plans to enhance its Auburn Hills headquarters and build its next e-Mobility Innovation Center.
TI Fluid Systems introduced Thermoplastic Refrigerant Lines (TPRL) that replace conventional aluminum and rubber assemblies and offer up to 60% weight reduction.
TI Fluid Systems received an EU grant for innovation at its Jablonec, Czech Republic facility.
TI Fluid Systems opened its first E-Mobility Innovation Center (eMIC) in Rastatt, Germany as a collaborative space for next-generation development.
TI Fluid Systems enrolled in DTE Energy’s MI Green Power Program to purchase 100% renewable energy.
On November 30, 2021, TI Fluid Systems announced it would supply a wide range of fluid products for global electric vehicle platforms from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
Hans Dieltjens succeeded William L. Kozyra as President and Chief Executive Officer on October 1, 2021.
On July 8, 2021, TI Fluid Systems announced it would supply thermal systems for the Jeep Wrangler 4xe from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On June 8, 2021, TI Fluid Systems launched new fluid handling technology for the 2021 Hyundai Santa Fe SUV Hybrid Electric Vehicle from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On May 28, 2021, TI Fluid Systems introduced integrated thermal manifold assemblies specifically designed for electric vehicles from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On February 17, 2021, William L. Kozyra announced his retirement as President & CEO, with EVP Hans Dieltjens preparing for succession.
On January 29, 2021, TI Fluid Systems launched innovative HEV plastic fuel tank technology with Volkswagen from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On December 15, 2020, TI Fluid Systems announced it would supply thermal coolant fluid carrying systems for Hyundai's BEV family of vehicles from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On November 5, 2020, TI Fluid Systems started high-volume production of industry-first thermal technology for Volkswagen ID.3 and ID.4 battery electric vehicles from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On July 27, 2020, TI Fluid Systems was awarded the London Stock Exchange’s Green Economy Mark.
On April 24, 2020, TI Fluid Systems announced a collaboration with Ford Motor Company and 3M to produce air purifying respirators in response to COVID-19 from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On March 4, 2020, TI Fluid Systems announced it would supply thermal products for the new Ford Mustang Mach-E full battery electric SUV from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On January 6, 2020, Ron Hundzinski was named Chief Financial Officer in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On May 2, 2019, TI Automotive rebranded as TI Fluid Systems in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On January 24, 2018, Stefan Rau was appointed Executive Vice President, Fluid Carrying Systems in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On January 22, 2018, TI Automotive announced it would provide blow-molded plastic tank technology for Karma Automotive’s luxury electric vehicle program from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
TI Automotive opened a new fuel tank systems production facility in Baoding, China to produce plastic fuel tank systems for Great Wall Motors Haval vehicles.
On June 3, 2016, Ford Motor Company honored TI Automotive as a top-performing global supplier at its 18th Annual World Excellence Awards in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On April 13, 2016, TI Automotive opened a new manufacturing facility in Monterrey, Mexico from Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On February 16, 2016, TI Automotive acquired Millennium Industries Corporation in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On January 4, 2016, TI Automotive moved its global corporate offices to Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On June 30, 2015, Bain Capital completed the acquisition of TI Automotive in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
On May 21, 2015, TI Automotive hosted students from Bullock Creek High School at its Technical Campus in Auburn Hills, Michigan to support STEM initiatives.
On January 27, 2015, TI Automotive signed a definitive agreement to be acquired by Bain Capital, ensuring stable ownership and commitment to technology investment.
TI Automotive won the 2014 Automotive News PACE Award for Tank Advanced Process Technology (TAPT), an adaptable plastic fuel tank production process for all vehicle powertrain systems.
TI Automotive opened a new fuel tank systems production facility in Guangzhou, China, marking its second blow-molding fuel tank facility and 15th facility within China.
At the 2014 North American International Auto Show, TI Automotive technology was featured on nearly two-thirds of new vehicles and powertrains.
TI Automotive brought its new Plastic Fuel Tank Advanced Process Technology (TAPT) into volume production, expanding upon the award-winning Ship-in-a-Bottle process.
On April 7, 2025, an operator at the Wyszków plant suffered a sudden cardiac arrest near hot-air ovens and was saved by first responders within 90 seconds.
TI Fluid Systems hosted an International Women’s Day roundtable with female engineers, including Dhivya Srinivasan, Director of Transformation.
TI Fluid Systems launched its 12V Electric Coolant Pump (eCP), tailored for electric vehicle thermal management.
TI Fluid Systems published a blog on 2025 lightweighting trends specifically highlighting next steps in automotive design for electric vehicles.
TI Fluid Systems outlined its approach to supporting all propulsion types, including hydrogen, to balance sustainability targets with consumer demands.
TI Fluid Systems discussed fuel tank adaptations for the hybrid-driven market as hybrid vehicles evolve into a sophisticated segment.
TI Fluid Systems delivered comprehensive product training sessions for its team between May and July.

facts: TI Fluid Systems plc is a global industry leader in highly engineered automotive fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems and thermal management products and systems.
The Group announced its full year results for the period ended 31 December 2024 on 11 March 2025.
Full year 2024 revenue was €3,360.3 million, a 4.4% decline year-on-year or 3.5% at constant currency.
Adjusted EBIT for 2024 was €260.8 million, representing a 40 basis points margin expansion to 7.8%.
Adjusted Free Cash Flow was €113.0 million, with a conversion rate of 29% of Adjusted EBITDA.
Total bookings for 2024 remained strong at €2.7 billion, including €0.5 billion in BEV awards and €0.6 billion in HEV awards.
Bookings demonstrated a clear shift to ICE vehicles, with ICE bookings representing over half of total awards in the period.
The company estimates that BEVs require 4-5 times as many connectors and 2-4 times as many coolant lines versus a comparable ICE vehicle.
TI Fluid Systems opened its fifth and final e-Mobility Innovation Centre (eMIC) in the US in the middle of 2024.
The company completed the development of its electric coolant pump (eCP) and installed its first manufacturing line in China ahead of industrialisation and launch in 2025.
TI Fluid Systems finalized and launched new product lines to underpin its EV strategy, including the SPT 2.0 fuel tank for plug-in hybrids.
The Group closed three facilities and downsized others during 2024 as part of footprint optimisation.
Restructuring costs increased to €37.4 million in 2024, largely related to headcount and footprint optimisation in response to softer industry volumes.
An exceptional charge was recorded primarily due to the closure of the Group's manufacturing facility in Ligonier and the associated exit of the powertrain business at that location.
The pending acquisition by ABC Technologies is expected to complete during H1 2025, subject to regulatory and court approvals.
Hans Dieltjens serves as Chief Executive Officer and President of TI Fluid Systems plc.
Julien Plenchette joined TI in April 2024 as President for the Americas region.
Renee McLeod was promoted to Chief Human Resources Officer in June 2024, and Samantha Bellis was promoted to Vice President, Operational Excellence in September 2024.
The company returned a total of €69.2 million to shareholders in 2024 via dividends and share buybacks, its largest single-year return since its 2017 IPO.
TI Fluid Systems aims for a 2026 revenue target of €3.8-4.2 billion and a >€4.5 billion target by 2030.
The company targets double-digit Adjusted EBIT margins in the mid-term and an Adjusted Free Cash Flow Conversion of 30%.
TI Fluid Systems achieved a 28% reduction in Scope 1 & 2 carbon emissions compared to its 2021 baseline, with a target of 50% reduction by 2030.
Demand for thermal products for EVs increased in the second half of 2024 as customers ramped up BEV production ahead of 2025 European CO2 emissions standards.
In China, TI Fluid Systems secured eight new awards with the largest Chinese OEM (LOEM) for brake lines and tanks for BEV and PHEV platforms.
The company launched circa 66 programmes during 2024, almost two thirds of which were with Local OEMs in China.

Americas revenue declined 8.1% at constant currency in 2024, impacted by destocking from the region's largest OEM customer and a circa 350 basis points headwind from exiting an unprofitable product line.
The Group maintains a propulsion agnostic portfolio to capture opportunities across ICE, PHEV, and BEV transitions.
source_title: MFN.se > TI Fluid Systems > Full Year Results 2024

facts: Critical Metals Corp. (Nasdaq: CRML) announced the commencement of its planned 10,000-meter diamond drilling campaign at its wholly owned Tanbreez Rare Earth Project in Southern Greenland.
The drill program is designed to support resource expansion, geological modeling, mine planning, and future development activities across the project area.
Over the past several weeks, the Company completed construction and commissioning of initial project infrastructure, including accommodation and operational support facilities.
The completed infrastructure supports both the 2026 drilling program and the continued advancement of the Tanbreez Project toward development.
Drill contractors and support teams successfully mobilized to site, with drill rigs arriving and positioned across priority target areas.
Following equipment inspections, safety reviews, and operational readiness assessments, the Company’s 2026 diamond drilling program is now underway.
Tony Sage serves as Chairman of CRML.
Tony Sage stated that the start of the 10,000-meter drilling campaign marks another important step in advancing the world-class Tanbreez rare earth Project.
Drilling is currently underway across priority target areas following a successful mobilization effort.
Information generated from this program is expected to support resource expansion, geological modelling, mine planning and other activities aimed at advancing Tanbreez toward production.
Over the coming two to three weeks, field teams will focus on final ground verification activities and completion of blast mark-outs associated with planned site access and development works.
These upcoming activities are designed to validate drill collar locations, optimize access corridors and support the safe and efficient execution of the 2026 drilling program.
The Company is advancing geological mapping and surface verification activities.
The Company is finalizing drill pads, access routes and supporting site infrastructure.
The Company is completing detailed collar surveys and positioning.
The Company is conducting ongoing environmental, health and safety monitoring programs.
The Company is expanding geological and technical data collection to support future resource updates, mine planning and project development activities.
Initial drilling will focus on priority mineralized zones identified through historical exploration, recent technical reviews, and geological modeling.
Results of the program are expected to support resource expansion, mine planning, infrastructure design and other workstreams associated with advancing Tanbreez toward production.
Tanbreez is one of the world’s largest undeveloped rare earth projects.
The project contains significant concentrations of heavy rare earth elements that are critical to permanent magnets, electric vehicles, defense systems and advanced manufacturing technologies.

The 2026 drilling campaign is expected to generate important geological and engineering data that will support future resource updates, mine planning, feasibility studies and broader project development activities.
The program will further enhance the Company’s understanding of the mineralization and geological characteristics across the project area.
The Company expects to provide additional operational updates as drilling progresses and assay results become available.

Critical Metals Corp is a leading mining development company focused on critical metals and minerals, producing strategic products essential to electrification and next generation technologies for Europe and its western world partners.
Its flagship Project, Tanbreez, is one of the world’s largest rare earth deposits located in Southern Greenland.
The Tanbreez deposit is expected to have access to key transportation outlets via year-round direct shipping access through deep water fjords leading directly to the North Atlantic Ocean.
Another key asset for the Company is the Wolfsberg Lithium Project located in Carinthia, 270 km south of Vienna, Austria.
The Wolfsberg Lithium Project is the first fully permitted mine in Europe.
The Wolfsberg project is strategically located with access to established road and rail infrastructure.
The Wolfsberg project is expected to be the next major producer of key lithium products to support the European market.
Wolfsberg is well positioned with offtake and downstream partners to become a unique and valuable asset in an expanding geostrategic critical metals portfolio.

With its strategic asset portfolio, Critical Metals Corp is positioned to become a reliable and sustainable supplier of critical minerals essential for defense applications, the clean energy transition, and next-generation technologies in the western world.
source_title: CRML Commences a 10,000 Meter Drilling Campaign at Tanbreez Rare Earth Project, Greenland - Critical Metals Corp

facts: Bosch is the largest automotive supplier in the world.
From 2025 onwards, it faced economic issues due to a decline in global German auto sales.
In 2015, Bosch purchased Seeo, Inc, a start-up working on solid-state lithium-ion batteries.
In June 2008, Bosch formed SB LiMotive, a 50:50 joint company with Samsung SDI.

The company held a ceremony for a 28.000m2 lithium-ion battery cell manufacturing plant in September 2009 and it is scheduled to start production for hybrid vehicles in 2011 and for electric vehicles in 2012.
Daimler AG and Bosch established a 50:50 joint venture (JV) to develop and manufacture electric motors in 2011.
The JV, called EM-motive GmbH, manufactures traction motors for electric, fuel cell and extended-range vehicles at a facility in Hildesheim, Germany.

In July 2022, Bosch announced plans to invest €3billion into its semiconductor chip production and research and development (R&D) over the next four years, including opening new facilities for manufacturing a computer chip development in Dresden and Reutlingen.
In September 2023, Bosch acquired TSI Semiconductors, a Roseville-headquartered silicon carbide (SiC) power device manufacturer.
The mobility division is the largest, in terms of revenue. The Bosch 2024 global annual report, showed that this division accounted for 61% of global revenues of €90.3billion.

This division creates solutions for vehicles and fleet management, developing software, vehicle electronics, logistics management solutions, driver assistance and safety systems and vehicle energy systems such as thermal performance.
The mobility division is active in powertrain systems, but has evolved to include hydrogen storage, electric propulsion, automated driving and connectivity.
Bosch Mobility is developing advanced driver assistance system software, cameras and sensors that they are overlaying with artificial intelligence.
In 2025, this divisions production was affected by geo-political decisions that led to exports of Nexperia's finished products being banned, which affected the supply chain of the automotive sector.

By December 2025 Bosch Mobility was announcing an AI extension platform, developed with technology partners, that allows existing vehicle cockpit systems to be upgraded to incorporate advanced AI without the requirement to change existing hardware and system infrastructure.

In 2025, Bosch Rexroth was partnering with Hyroad Energy and GenH2, to develop a zero-loss liquid hydrogen refueling station in the United States, which will utilise Bosch Rexroth's Liquid Hydrogen CryoPump technology.
Bosch is also a manufacturer of electric bicycles, offering motors and systems connected to e-bikes such as batteries, displays, and digital services.
In September 2025, the company announced plans to cut 13,000 jobs at its auto parts business, representing 3% of its global workforce, in stages by 2030 as it relies more on artificial intelligence.

In January 2026, the company was reported to be among the top 30 global investors into R&D in 2025, while the Bosch Group's profits had reduced by almost half in the same fiscal year, with more than 20,000 job cuts in Germany by 2030, primarily in the mobility division.

Factories and distribution facilities are located in Mt. Prospect, Illinois; Hoffman Estates, Illinois; Broadview, Illinois; Kentwood, Michigan; Warren, Michigan; Owatonna, Minnesota; Waltham, Massachusetts; Clarksville, Tennessee; Anderson, South Carolina; Charleston, South Carolina; New Bern, North Carolina; and 11 other cities.
In 2015, Bosch takes over 100% of the shares of the former ZF Lenksysteme (Steering Systems) GmbH joint venture (was 50/50 with ZF Friedrichshafen).

In 2020, Bosch funded the creation of a report entitled Decarbonising Road Transport: There Is No Silver Bullet, which contained disputed information about the environmental performance of electric vehicles.
In January 2021, Volkswagen filed a US$1.2-billion class-action against Bosch and Continental AG in the United States after VW was forced to reduce production due to a lack of automotive microchips.
In June 2021, Bosch opened a newly built semiconductor manufacturing plant in which it invested US$1.2billion, its largest-ever spending on a single project.
In April 2022, Bosch acquired Five.ai, a vehicular automation startup.

Bosch won the German Future Prize again in late 2025, for developing a zero-emission fuel-cell power module to power heavy vehicles. The system transforms hydrogen and oxygen to electrical energy, emitting only water vapor, when using green hydrogen.
source_title: Bosch (company) - Wikipedia

facts: Headquartered in Duluth, Ga., Fox Factory Holding Corp. reports financial results for the fourth fiscal quarter ended January 3, 2025.
Net sales for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 were $353 million, up $20 million over prior year.
Gross margin achieved 28.9%, up 120 basis points over prior year.
Adjusted gross margin increased by 20 basis points to 29.2% from prior year.
Earnings per diluted share for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 was breakeven.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share was $0.31.
Drove sequential revenue growth and EBITDA margin improvement in both AAG and PVG segments.
Bike revenues grew 8.3% over prior year.
Working capital improvements generated $63 million in debt paydown.
Cost reduction initiatives underway and progressing in line with expectations.
Mike Dennison serves as FOX’s Chief Executive Officer.
CEO Mike Dennison stated end markets remain uneven and challenging to navigate, expecting this backdrop to continue into 2025.
Management remains focused on controllable elements through operational improvements and strategic cost management initiatives.
Actions to improve working capital, strengthen OE partnerships and dealer relationships, and streamline operations are beginning to yield results.
Improved cash generation allowed paying down debt balance by $63 million in the fourth quarter.
Broader cost and operational initiatives are progressing well, with benefits beginning to flow through toward a $25 million target.
Net sales for Q4 FY2024 were $352.8 million, an increase of 6.1% compared to $332.5 million in Q4 FY2023.

Increase reflects a $31.1 million (33.3%) increase in Specialty Sports Group (SSG), partially offset by an $8.6 million (7.1%) decrease in Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG) and a $2.2 million (1.8%) decrease in Powered Vehicles Group (PVG).

SSG net sales increased from $93.4 million to $124.5 million, primarily due to inclusion of a full-quarter net sales of $41.5 million from Marucci (acquired Nov 2023) and a $6.4 million increase in bike sales.
Ongoing channel inventory recalibration and lower end consumer demand remain headwinds for bike sales.

AAG net sales decreased from $120.8 million to $112.2 million, driven by lower upfitting sales due to product mix, chassis availability, higher interest rates impacting dealers and consumers, and higher inventory levels at dealerships.
AAG segment generated growth of 11.9% on a sequential basis from the third quarter reflecting strategic initiatives to improve performance.
PVG net sales decreased from $118.3 million to $116.2 million, primarily due to lower industry demand in power sports and automotive because of higher interest rates and higher inventory levels.
Gross margin was 28.9% for Q4 FY2024, a 120-basis point increase from 27.7% in Q4 FY2023.
Increase in gross margin primarily due to amortization of acquired inventory valuation markup from the Marucci acquisition in prior year, fully recognized by end of Q1 FY2024.
Total operating expenses were $90.6 million (25.7% of net sales) for Q4 FY2024, compared to $81.0 million (24.4%) in Q4 FY2023.
Operating expenses increased by $9.6 million primarily driven by recognition of a full quarter of Marucci operating expenses following Nov 2023 acquisition.
Adjusted operating expenses were $76.4 million (21.7% of net sales) for Q4 FY2024, compared to $68.5 million (20.6%) in prior year.
Tax benefit was $4.1 million in Q4 FY2024, compared to tax benefit of $3.1 million in Q4 FY2023.
Net loss attributable to FOX stockholders in Q4 FY2024 was $0.1 million, compared to net income of $4.1 million in prior year.
Loss per diluted share for Q4 FY2024 was $0.00, compared to earnings per diluted share of $0.10 in prior year.
Adjusted net income in Q4 FY2024 was $12.8 million ($0.31 adjusted EPS), compared to $20.3 million ($0.48 adjusted EPS) in prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 FY2024 was $40.4 million, compared to $38.8 million in prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA margin in Q4 FY2024 was 11.5%, compared to 11.7% in prior year.
Net sales for fiscal year ended Jan 3, 2025 were $1,393.9 million, a decrease of 4.8% compared to fiscal 2023.

Fiscal year decrease reflects a $129.6 million (23.5%) decrease in AAG net sales and a $62.5 million (11.9%) decrease in PVG net sales, partially offset by a $121.9 million (31.3%) increase in SSG net sales.
Gross margin was 30.4% in fiscal year 2024, a 130-basis point decrease compared to 31.7% in fiscal year 2023.
Decrease in gross margin for FY2024 primarily driven by shifts in product line mix and operating leverage on lower volume.
Total operating expenses were $365.9 million (26.3% of net sales) for FY2024, compared to $304.7 million (20.8%) in FY2023.
Operating expenses increased by $61.2 million primarily due to inclusion of Marucci operating expenses of $78.7 million, partially offset by cost controls.
Net income attributable to FOX stockholders in FY2024 was $6.6 million, compared to $120.8 million in prior fiscal year.
Earnings per diluted share for FY2024 was $0.16, compared to $2.85 in same period of FY2023.
Adjusted net income in FY2024 was $55.4 million ($1.33 adjusted EPS), compared to $167.5 million ($3.95 adjusted EPS) in prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA decreased to $167.0 million in FY2024, compared to $261.0 million in FY2023.
Adjusted EBITDA margin decreased to 12.0% in FY2024, compared to 17.8% in FY2023.
As of Jan 3, 2025, Company had cash and cash equivalents of $71.7 million, compared to $83.6 million as of Dec 29, 2023.
Inventory was $404.7 million as of Jan 3, 2025, compared to $371.8 million as of Dec 29, 2023.
As of Jan 3, 2025, accounts receivable and accounts payable were $165.8 million and $144.1 million, respectively.
Prepaids and other current assets were $85.4 million as of Jan 3, 2025, compared to $141.5 million as of Dec 29, 2023.
Decrease in cash and cash equivalents driven by Marzocchi acquisition, debt payments, and capital expenditures.

Inventory increased by $32.9 million driven by higher raw materials and finished goods due to imbalance in expected versus fulfilled orders and intentional build of high moving stocking units in aftermarket businesses.
Total debt was $705.1 million as of Jan 3, 2025, an improvement of $38.4 million compared to $743.5 million in prior year period.
Working capital improvements, especially reduction in chassis prepayments, drove debt paydown as company continues to focus on generating free cash flow to reduce debt and interest expense.
For Q1 FY2025, Company expects net sales in range of $320 million to $350 million and adjusted EPS in range of $0.12 to $0.32.
For FY2025, Company expects net sales in range of $1.385 billion to $1.485 billion, adjusted EPS in range of $1.60 to $2.60, and full year adjusted tax rate in range of 15% to 18%.
Guidance does not include any effects from ongoing tariff developments.
Company is a direct supplier of shocks, suspension, and components to leading powered vehicle and bicycle original equipment manufacturers.
Company offers premium baseball and softball gear and equipment.
Company acquires complementary businesses to integrate engineering and manufacturing expertise to reach beyond core shock and suspension segment.
Provides products in aftermarket through global network of retailers and distributors and through direct-to-consumer channels.

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: FOXF) is a premium brand and global leader in the design, engineering, and manufacturing of performance-defining products and systems for customers worldwide.
The company reported financial results for the first fiscal quarter ended April 3, 2026.
First Quarter Fiscal 2026 net sales increased 3.9% year-over-year to $368.7 million, reaching the high end of guidance.
The company reported a net loss of $15.0 million, or $0.36 per diluted share, which included charges associated with divestitures in its AAG segment.
This compares to a net loss of $259.7 million, or $6.23 per diluted share, in the prior year quarter which included goodwill impairment.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share were $0.18, compared to $0.23 in the prior year quarter.
Adjusted EBITDA was $35.7 million, exceeding the high end of the guidance range.
The company reaffirmed approximately $50 million of fiscal 2026 cost savings, with Phase 2 actions on schedule.
Fox Factory completed the divestiture of its Phoenix, Arizona AAG operations, including the Shock Therapy, Upfit UTV, and Geiser businesses, with proceeds dedicated to debt reduction.
Mike Dennison serves as FOX’s Chief Executive Officer.
CEO Mike Dennison stated that revenue was at the high end of guidance and adjusted EBITDA exceeded the high end of guidance.
The company is executing against a plan laid out in February to take cost out, sharpen the portfolio, and build resilience in a subdued end-market environment.
Phase 1 carryover benefits are flowing through as expected, and Phase 2 is on schedule to deliver approximately $50 million in cost savings this year.
The completion of the Phoenix divestiture focuses the portfolio on core, higher-margin businesses that define Fox Factory.
Combined with disciplined capital allocation and balance sheet management, these actions position the company to deliver meaningful operating leverage as end markets accelerate.
Net sales for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 were $368.7 million, an increase of 3.9% compared to net sales of $355.0 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
The sales increase reflects a $21.3 million or 17.4% increase in Powered Vehicles Group (PVG) net sales, and a $2.9 million or 2.6% increase in Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG) net sales.
This was partially offset by a $10.5 million or 8.7% decrease in Specialty Sports Group (SSG) net sales.
The PVG net sales increase from $122.1 million to $143.4 million is mainly attributed to strengthening demand in powersports and continued momentum in the automotive aftermarket.
The AAG net sales increase from $111.9 million to $114.8 million is driven by improved performance in upfitting product lines and stable aftermarket product sales.

The SSG net sales decrease from $121.0 million to $110.5 million primarily reflects distributor and dealer inventory destocking and a difficult prior-year comparison given the industry’s first half 2025 order pull-forward.
Gross margin was 28.9% for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, compared to gross margin of 30.9% in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
The decrease in gross margin was primarily driven by the net impact of tariffs and shifts in product line mix.
Total operating expenses were $100.4 million, or 27.2% of net sales, for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, compared to $360.3 million, or 101.5% of net sales in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
Operating expenses decreased by $259.8 million, driven primarily by a $262.1 million goodwill impairment recorded in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

Adjusted operating expenses were $85.5 million, or 23.2% of net sales, in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, compared to $84.4 million, or 23.8% of net sales, in the first quarter of the prior fiscal year.
Other expense, net for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 was $9.6 million, an increase of $9.8 million from $0.1 million other income, net in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
The increase in other expense, net was primarily attributable to a $10.0 million loss on divestiture of the Phoenix, Arizona AAG operations.
Income tax benefit was $0.6 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, compared to $3.6 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

The difference between the Company’s effective tax rate of 3.9% and the 21% federal statutory rate was primarily attributable to lower pre-tax earnings and tax effects from the sale of Phoenix, Arizona AAG operations.

Net loss attributable to FOX stockholders in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 was $15.0 million, compared to net loss attributable to FOX stockholders of $259.7 million in the first quarter of the prior fiscal year.

Adjusted net income in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 was $7.4 million, or $0.18 of adjusted earnings per diluted share, compared to adjusted net income of $9.8 million, or $0.23 of adjusted earnings per diluted share, in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted EBITDA margin in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 was 9.7%, compared to 11.2% in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
As of April 3, 2026, the Company had cash and cash equivalents of $53.9 million, compared to $58.0 million as of January 2, 2026.
Inventory was $375.1 million as of April 3, 2026, compared to $388.6 million as of January 2, 2026.
As of April 3, 2026, accounts receivable and accounts payable were $209.1 million and $143.4 million, respectively, compared to $190.7 million and $141.4 million, respectively, as of January 2, 2026.
Prepaids and other current assets were $126.3 million as of April 3, 2026, compared to $108.4 million as of January 2, 2026.
Goodwill was $83.6 million as of April 3, 2026, compared to $83.6 million as of January 2, 2026.
Total debt was $688.2 million as of April 3, 2026 compared to $673.5 million as of January 2, 2026.
In May, the Company proactively amended its credit agreement to provide additional financial flexibility, including the expansion of the net leverage covenant to 5.0x, compared to the prior 4.5x.
The decrease in cash and cash equivalents was mainly due to changes in working capital, debt payments, and capital expenditures, partially offset by proceeds from its revolver.
Inventory decreased by $13.5 million, driven by divested inventory.
The increase in accounts receivable is due to higher sales in the fiscal quarter ended April 3, 2026 compared to the fiscal quarter ended January 2, 2026 and timing of collections.
The increase in accounts payable reflects the timing of vendor payments.
The increase in prepaids and other assets is mainly attributable to receivables arising from the divestiture of its Phoenix, Arizona AAG operations.

Fox Factory continues to execute against a multi-phase profit optimization strategy first outlined in its fourth quarter fiscal 2025 earnings release, which targets approximately $50 million of realized savings in fiscal 2026.

This includes approximately $10 million of carryover benefit from the Phase 1 program completed in fiscal 2025 and approximately $40 million of incremental savings from Phase 2 actions initiated earlier this year.

Phase 2 comprises three strategic elements: business line rationalization to exit operations that are not accretive from a margin perspective; supply chain and materials cost productivity through improved facility utilization and supplier actions; and a reduction in operating expenses across sales, marketing, and G&A functions.
The Company continues to evaluate strategic alternatives for its other non-core assets to ensure the portfolio aligns with profitability standards and strategic objectives.
For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, the Company expects net sales in the range of $343 million to $365 million.
For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, the Company expects adjusted EBITDA in the range of $32 million to $40 million.
For the fiscal year 2026, the Company is reaffirming net sales guidance in the range of $1.328 billion to $1.416 billion.
For the fiscal year 2026, the Company is reaffirming adjusted EBITDA guidance in the range of $174 million to $203 million.
The Company has visibility to potential recoveries of certain tariff costs previously incurred under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) framework.
Any potential tariff cost recoveries are subject to significant uncertainty regarding timing, amount, and allocation among the Company and its commercial counterparties.
The Company will recognize tariff recovery amounts only upon receipt and has not included any potential recovery in its outlook.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. is a direct supplier of shocks, suspension, and components to leading powered vehicle and bicycle original equipment manufacturers.
The company offers premium baseball and softball gear and equipment through its portfolio of brands like FOX, Marucci, Method Race Wheels, and more.
The company provides products in the aftermarket through its global network of retailers and distributors and through direct-to-consumer channels.

Fox Factory Holding Corp. announces material information to the public through filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, press releases, public conference calls, webcasts, and the Investor Relations section of its website.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Q4 FY2025 net income attributable to common shareholders was $294 million, up 77% YoY.
Q4 FY2025 adjusted EBITDA was $473 million, down 8% YoY but up 29% QoQ.
Q4 FY2025 rolled product shipments were 957 kilotonnes, up 1% YoY.
Full FY2025 net income attributable to common shareholders was $683 million, up 14% YoY.
Full FY2025 adjusted EBITDA was $1.8 billion, down 4% YoY.
Full FY2025 rolled product shipments were 3,757 kilotonnes, up 2% YoY.
Novelis maintained the recycled content of its products at 63% in fiscal 2025.
Q4 FY2025 net sales increased 13% to $4.6 billion, driven by higher average aluminum prices and a 1% increase in total rolled product shipments.
Higher demand for beverage packaging, aerospace, and specialty products was partially offset by lower automotive shipments in Q4 FY2025.
Full FY2025 net sales increased 6% to $17.1 billion, driven by higher average aluminum prices and a 2% increase in total flat rolled product shipments.

The increase in full year shipments is mainly due to record high beverage packaging shipments and higher shipments for aerospace products, partially offset by lower shipments of specialties and automotive products.
Fiscal 2025 net cash flow provided by operating activities was $1.0 billion compared to $1.3 billion in the prior fiscal year.
Adjusted Free Cash Flow was an outflow of $737 million in fiscal 2025 compared to a prior year period outflow of $75 million.

Fiscal year 2025 capital expenditures totaled $1.7 billion, reflecting planned increases in strategic, sustainability-focused capital investment projects supporting increased long-term customer demand.
In January 2025, Novelis issued $750 million in senior unsecured notes due January 2030, primarily used to repay outstanding borrowings under its ABL revolver.
In March 2025, the company borrowed $1.25 billion of term loans maturing in March 2032, primarily used to repay previously issued term loans due in 2026 and 2028.
The company had a net leverage ratio of 2.9x at the end of Q4 FY2025.
Total liquidity stood at $2.8 billion as of March 31, 2025, consisting of $1.0 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $1.7 billion in availability under committed credit facilities.

Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.

Novelis' ambition is to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and to achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers in aerospace, automotive, beverage packaging, and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Restructuring and impairment expenses for the twelve months ended March 31, 2025 include $21 million related to the closure of the Buckhannon, West Virginia plant.
Steve Fisher is president and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Dev Ahuja is executive vice president and CFO of Novelis Inc.

facts: Stryten Energy is the only company with a complete offering of advanced lead, lithium and vanadium batteries.

facts: Ascend Elements achieved a milestone producing >99% pure, recycled lithium carbonate (Li2CO3) from black mass for the first time at commercial scale at its Covington, Ga. facility on September 3, 2025.
The company plans to produce more than 15 Kilotons of Recycled Lithium Carbonate annually in the United States and Europe by 2027.
On December 10, 2024, Ascend Elements announced it would increase U.S. production of lithium carbonate by 60% with the start of a new lithium recovery line at its Covington, Ga. facility in 2025.
The new lithium recovery line at the Covington, Ga. facility will produce up to 3,000 metric tons of lithium carbonate (Li2CO3) annually.
The Covington, Ga. EV battery recycling facility was certified to ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Standard by DQS Inc.
Base 1 is Ascend Elements’ first commercial-scale EV battery recycling facility located in Covington, Georgia.
When fully operational in Q4 2022, Base 1 will recycle 30,000 metric tons of lithium-ion batteries and manufacturing scrap a year, equivalent to about 70,000 electric vehicle batteries per year.
The Covington, Ga. facility was planned to open by August 2022 as a 154,000-square-foot commercial-scale lithium-ion battery recycling facility with capacity to process 30,000 metric tons annually.

Ascend Elements will recycle lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap for SK Battery America at its Covington, Georgia facility, recovering 98% of battery materials and returning critical elements to the supply chain.
The company plans to invest up to $1 billion to build a sustainable lithium-ion battery materials facility in Hopkinsville, Ky.

Ascend Elements began construction of its largest EV battery recycling and engineered materials manufacturing facility (Apex 1) in southwestern Kentucky with a ceremonial groundbreaking attended by Gov. Andy Beshear.
The Apex 1 campus is designed as a 150-acre site for America’s first commercial-scale pCAM manufacturing plant.
Ascend Elements launched a logistics simulation at its Apex 1 facility currently under construction in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, selecting EY US to develop the complex model.
A separate $316M U.S. DOE grant to Ascend Elements for pCAM infrastructure remains active to help fund construction of the Apex 1 project in Hopkinsville, KY.
The company was awarded $480M in grants from the U.S. Department of Energy’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Law Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing Initiative.
A separate $164M DOE grant for Cathode Active Material (CAM) was mutually canceled due to changing market conditions.

Ascend Elements secured an up to $5 billion deal to supply sustainable cathode precursor (pCAM) made from recycled battery materials to a major U.S. customer while building one of North America’s first commercial-scale NMC pCAM manufacturing facilities in southwest Kentucky.

The company's Hydro-to-Cathode™ technology recovers 98% of critical elements in used lithium-ion batteries and uses microstructure engineering techniques to adjust the elemental crystal structure of new cathode material to precise customer specifications.

An independently reviewed Life Cycle Assessment found Ascend Elements’ Hydro-to-Cathode® direct pCAM synthesis process produces 49% lower carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emissions than traditional battery recycling methods.

The company announced a multi-year offtake agreement with Trafigura to pair its Hydro-to-Cathode® technology with Trafigura’s global marketing and logistics platform to accelerate a resilient battery materials supply chain.

Ascend Elements and Koura (Orbia’s Fluorinated Solutions business) agreed to explore joint commercialization of the patented Hydro-to-Anode® graphite recycling technology, which produces 99.9% pure graphite from used lithium-ion batteries.
The company plans to supply up to 5,000 metric tons of recycled lithium carbonate per year to Koura Global for new electric vehicle batteries.

Ascend Elements and EcoPro Group announced an MOU to cooperate in the development, qualification, and integration of recycled battery materials into EcoPro’s cathode products, paving the way for a sustainable, closed-loop supply of domestic lithium-ion battery materials in the United States.
The company shipped decarbonized cathode materials to Freudenberg e-Power Systems, a leading supplier of emissions-neutral energy solutions for heavy-duty applications.

Ascend Elements produces premium cathode active materials for Navitas Systems, a U.S. leader in comprehensive energy storage solutions and a U.S. Department of Defense contractor, under a contract signed on January 31, 2022.
The company formalized an agreement with Call2Recycle to offer customized EV battery management, logistics, and recycling services to automotive dealerships, recyclers, and repair facilities.
Ascend Elements signed an agreement with Honda to recycle Honda & Acura Electric Vehicles (EV) batteries at its Worcester, Mass. facility.

The company raised $542 million in a 2023 equity round, one of the largest U.S. cleantech equity private placements of that year, to accelerate production of U.S.-engineered lithium-ion battery materials.
Ascend Elements raised an additional $162 million in new equity investments, adding to a recent $542 million round for a 12-month funding total of $704 million.
The company secured $300 million in equity and debt financing, including $200 million in Series C equity investments from an international group of strategic and financial investors.

Battery Resourcers (now Ascend Elements) raised $70 million in mid-round funding to scale commercial operations across two continents and develop a closed-loop approach to lithium-ion battery materials.

The company raised $20 million in a Series B equity round led by Orbia Ventures, with participation from At One Ventures, TDK Ventures, TRUMPF Venture, Doral Energy-Tech Ventures, and Jaguar Land Rover’s InMotion.
TDK Ventures was named Ascend Elements’ 2025 Partner of the Year for advancing growth, global expansion, and leadership in forging a local critical mineral refining strategy.

The company's co-founders, Eric Gratz, Ph.D. and Yan Wang, Ph.D., were recognized for their lithium-ion battery recycling process and won the AUTM Better World Project award for 2022 alongside Worcester Polytechnic Institute.
WPI Professor and co-founder Prof. Yan Wang was awarded a USABC grant to lead a $2 million project to lower the cost and improve the performance of recycled battery materials.
Linh Austin was appointed President and CEO on March 20, 2025, overseeing the company's evolution from an R&D startup to a commercial-scale manufacturer.

The company was founded in 2015 by co-founders who discovered a new way to recycle lithium-ion batteries and return critical battery metals to the domestic supply chain.
The company's patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis technology was purpose-built for environmental and economic sustainability.
Ascend Elements changed its name from Battery Resourcers to Ascend Elements on January 19, 2022, as part of a comprehensive brand refresh.
The company announced plans to increase processing capacity through strategic facility conversions and multiple expansions, originally announced on August 24, 2021.

CTO Eric Gratz, Ph.D. was recognized by Business Insider as an EV industry “Power Player” on a list of the 30 most influential leaders in the electric vehicle and Li-ion battery industries.
Popular Science featured Ascend Elements as a leading innovator in EV battery recycling, highlighting co-founder and CTO Eric Gratz demonstrating the Hydro-to-Cathode process.
Scientific American featured co-founder Yan Wang and his innovative Hydro-to-Cathode battery recycling process.

The Republic of Poland offered Ascend Elements up to USD $320 million in cash subsidy to support construction of a lithium-ion battery materials plant, one of the largest grants ever offered by Poland’s Ministry of Economic Development and Technology.
PAIH named Ascend Elements’ Project Apex 2 Manufacturing Investment of the Year for its EU-first battery material refining center using Hydro-to-Cathode® technology in Warsaw, Poland.

AE Elemental, a joint venture between U.S.-based Ascend Elements and Poland-based Elemental Strategic Metals, opened an advanced Li-ion EV battery recycling facility in Zawiercie, Poland, with another planned in central Germany.
The company closed 2025 with strong business momentum and commercial scale production of critical minerals for battery production, positioning itself for accelerated growth through 2026 and beyond.
